title: Autoscaling
helper: Automatically adjust the number of replicas based on demand to optimize resource usage and performance.
description: Automatically adjust the number of replicas based on demand to optimize resource usage and performance. Scaling up typically takes around 30 seconds, while the scale-down stabilization period is configured at 300 seconds.

scalingMetric.label: Scaling metric
scalingMetric.defaultDescription: Select a metric to determine when to scale.

descriptions.runningRequests: Scale based on the number of requests currently being processed.
descriptions.waitingRequests: Scale based on the number of requests waiting in queue.

aggregation.label: Aggregation
aggregation.description: How the metric values are aggregated over the evaluation period.

tooltips.avg: Average metric value across all pods. Best for stable, gradual scaling.
tooltips.max: Highest metric value across all pods. Reacts to peak load.
tooltips.min: Lowest metric value across all pods. Only scales when all pods are busy.

targetType.label: Target type
targetType.description: How the target value is interpreted for scaling decisions.

tooltips.value: Scale when the total metric value across all pods exceeds the target.
tooltips.averageValue: Scale when the average metric per pod exceeds the target. Best for request-based metrics.

descriptions.value: Scale up when metric exceeds this value.
descriptions.averageValue: Scale up when average metric per pod exceeds this value.
